title: 1.1: Identifies that SQL databases make no ordering guarantees for rows with identical ORDER BY values (e.g., nondeterministic behavior for tied sort keys)
1.2: Identifies the Document model ordering configuration uses a single non-unique field (e.g., ordering by created timestamp without a secondary tiebreaker)
1.3: States that DISTINCT does not fix the ordering stability issue (e.g., DISTINCT removes duplicate rows from JOINs but does not impose order on tied keys)
1.4: Identifies that tag filtering creates multiple JOINs when iterating over tag IDs (e.g., multiple INNER JOIN statements to the junction table)
1.5: Reports the generated SQL query structure showing pagination with ORDER BY and LIMIT/OFFSET (e.g., ORDER BY created DESC with LIMIT and OFFSET clauses)
1.6: Reports observable API behavior across consecutive page requests (e.g., showing page 1 and page 2 results with document IDs)
1.7: Identifies the source of identical sort key values across documents (e.g., documents created within the same millisecond during bulk operations)
1.8: Describes the frontend pagination assumptions (e.g., UI assumes page N+1 follows page N without overlap)
